key_insight: A mesh graph neural network can predict von Mises stress on 2D parts with different hole geometries, generalizing better than standard ML surrogates because it uses mesh topology and relative features instead of absolute coordinates.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster finite element screening of structural designs under multiple loads, reducing the cost of iterative geometry optimization and what-if analysis.
method: Encode mesh nodes by type: fixed boundary, free surface, and hole edge.
Use relative edge distances plus global load as inputs for translation- and rotation-invariant prediction.
Train a graph neural network on simulated FE data and compare against Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and KNN.
evidence_signal: Trained on 11 plate geometries under 20 load conditions and tested on 7 unseen geometries and 3 unseen loads, with the best case reaching R^2 >= 0.97 versus about 0.01–0.86 for conventional models.
watch_item: Performance may degrade on geometries or loading regimes farther outside the training distribution, so high-fidelity FE validation is still needed before design use.
paper_title: Mesh Graph Neural Network Framework for Accelerating Finite Element Simulation for Arbitrary Geometries
